documentation.description:
-
  Ada.Calendar starts at 1901 while the HP-41 time module start at 1900 which makes it necessary to account for
  that difference. Note that 1900 was not a leap year because of the 100 rule however 2000 was because of the 400
  rule.

record_types.name: Alarm_Type

subtypes.name: Repeat_Time
subtypes.code: subtype Repeat_Time is Duration range 1.0 .. Maximum_Repeat;

constants.name: Base_Time
constants.code:
Base_Time : constant Ada.Calendar.Time := Ada.Calendar.Formatting.Time_Of
      (Year        => Ada.Calendar.Year_Number'First,
       Month       => Ada.Calendar.Month_Number'First,
       Day         => Ada.Calendar.Day_Number'First,
       Hour        => Ada.Calendar.Formatting.Hour_Number'First,
       Minute      => Ada.Calendar.Formatting.Minute_Number'First,
       Second      => Ada.Calendar.Formatting.Second_Number'First,
       Sub_Second  => 0.0,
       Leap_Second => False,
       Time_Zone   => 0);

subprograms.name: From_Repeat
subprograms.code: function From_Repeat (Value : in Duration) return HP41_Duration

subprograms.name: From_Start
subprograms.code: function From_Start (Value : in Start_Time) return HP41_Duration

subprograms.name: From_Start
subprograms.code: function From_Start (Value : in Start_Time) return Duration

generic_instantiations.name: Alarm_Vectors
